footer.disclaimer: VisaForge is not a law firm. We do not provide legal advice.

home.title: VisaForge — Self-file your US visa with AI guidance
home.description: EB-2 NIW, O-1A, EB-1A and more. Templates, checklists, AI assistant. For ambitious individuals filing themselves.

hero.heading: Self-file your US visa with AI guidance
hero.subheading: EB-2 NIW, O-1A, EB-1A and more — templates, checklists, AI assistant. Built for individuals, not corporations.
hero.trustLine: We never give legal advice. We help you prepare your own petition.

problem.heading: Why this exists
problem.intro: Immigration lawyers charge $5,000 to $15,000 or more. Existing platforms target corporations. Nothing exists for ambitious individuals filing themselves.

bullets.cost: Attorney fees are prohibitive for early-career applicants
bullets.tools: Tools like Alma and Boundless serve HR teams, not individuals
bullets.diy: Self-filers face USCIS forms with no guidance and no feedback

howItWorks.heading: How it works

quiz.title: 1. Take the quiz
quiz.body: Five minutes — personalized visa path recommendation.

templates.title: 2. Use templates
templates.body: Petition letters, business plans, recommendation letter samples.

ai.title: 3. AI assistant
ai.body: Answers 24/7. Reviews your drafts. Suggests improvements.

howItWorks.disclaimerBanner: AI guidance is information, not legal advice.

visaTypes.heading: Visas we focus on

eb2niw.name: EB-2 NIW
eb2niw.desc: National Interest Waiver. For applicants whose work benefits the United States.

o1a.name: O-1A
o1a.desc: Extraordinary ability in sciences, business, education, athletics.

eb1a.name: EB-1A
eb1a.desc: Extraordinary ability — the highest skill category for green card.

visaTypes.alsoSupported: H-1B · L-1 · E-2 · F-1 / OPT → H-1B transition
visaTypes.asylumNote: Asylum guidance is paused due to current US policy.

pricing.heading: Pricing
pricing.subheading: Start free. Upgrade when you’re ready to file.
pricing.ctaNote: Be first to know when we launch

legalAdvice.a: No. VisaForge is not a law firm and we do not provide legal advice. Our templates and AI assistant are educational tools — for legal advice, consult a licensed immigration attorney.

lawyerReview.q: Can a real lawyer review my documents?
lawyerReview.a: Yes — on our Expert tier, a partner immigration attorney reviews your petition before you file. Optional and one-time per petition.

supportedVisas.a: Primary focus: EB-2 NIW, O-1A, EB-1A. Also supported: H-1B, L-1, E-2, and F-1/OPT to H-1B transitions.

policyChanges.a: We monitor USCIS rule changes weekly and update templates and checklists. We pause categories where guidance is unreliable (asylum is currently paused).

launch.a: Beta in Q3 2026 — waitlist members are invited first.

languages.a: EN and UK at launch. ES, ZH, and HI are on the roadmap based on waitlist demand.

finalCta.heading: Get on the waitlist
finalCta.subheading: Be among the first to file your US visa with AI guidance.

testimonials.heading: Early-access voices
testimonials.subheading: Notes from people we're building this with.

quotes.andrii-k: The petition-letter template was the first time I understood what evidence USCIS actually expects from me.
quotes.maria-g: Five-minute quiz mapped O-1A vs EB-1A side by side, with concrete evidence buckets for each. Huge head-start.
quotes.olena-p: The AI flagged three weak sentences in my NIW letter I'd have shipped without thinking.
quotes.raj-s: Document checklist saved me three weekends of digging through Reddit threads.
quotes.dmytro-l: Found out my H-1B path could pivot to NIW. Hadn't even considered it.
quotes.sofia-c: Recommendation-letter samples were specific to creative work — not just generic engineer templates.
quotes.volodymyr-b: Asked the AI to critique my draft as if it were a USCIS officer. The rewrite suggestions were surgical.
quotes.anastasiia-r: Honest about what AI does and doesn't do — not pretending to be a lawyer. That trust mattered.
quotes.marcus-t: Was paying a $400/hr consultant for the same checklist VisaForge has built in.
quotes.yulia-m: Started out thinking I'd need a lawyer. Finished my draft over two weekends.

notLawFirm.heading: Not a law firm
notLawFirm.body: VisaForge is not a law firm. Using this website or any of our tools does not create an attorney-client relationship. We do not provide legal advice. Information on this site is provided for general educational purposes only.

aiOutputs.heading: AI outputs are information, not advice
aiOutputs.body: Our AI assistant generates educational information based on publicly available USCIS guidance. AI outputs may contain errors, omissions, or outdated information. Do not rely on AI outputs as a substitute for legal advice from a licensed immigration attorney.

uscisRules.heading: USCIS rules change frequently
uscisRules.body: United States immigration law and USCIS policies change frequently. Always verify any information against the official USCIS website (uscis.gov) and current law before submitting any petition or relying on guidance.

recommendation.heading: We strongly recommend a licensed attorney
recommendation.body: For any specific legal question — including whether you qualify for a particular visa, how to interpret regulations, or how to respond to USCIS — we strongly recommend consulting a licensed US immigration attorney.

liability.heading: Limitation of liability
liability.body: VisaForge is provided "as is" without warranty of any kind. To the maximum extent permitted by law, we disclaim liability for any damages arising from your use of this site or its content, including but not limited to denied petitions, missed deadlines, or rejected applications.

resources.heading: Useful official resources
resources.aila: AILA — find a licensed immigration attorney
